text: Objective response was achieved in 52.3% vs 16.3% (difference, 36 percentage points; 95% CI, 28 to 44; P<0.001) with trastuzumab deruxtecan.
Pathologic complete response was achieved in 64.8% vs 51.2% (difference, 13.6 percentage points; 95% CI, 5.4 to 21.8; P<0.001) with pembrolizumab.
Objective response was achieved in 56% vs 46% (difference, 10 percentage points; 95% CI, 2 to 18) with atezolizumab.
Objective response was achieved in 42% vs 27% (difference, 15 percentage points; 95% CI, 8 to 22; P<0.001) with nivolumab-ipilimumab.
Response rate was 44.8% vs 27.8% (difference, 17 percentage points; 95% CI, 6 to 28; P=0.001) with pembrolizumab.
Objective response was achieved in 43.7% vs 33.4% (difference, 10.3 percentage points; 95% CI, 3.7 to 16.9) with durvalumab-tremelimumab.
Objective response was achieved in 27.3% vs 5.5% (difference, 21.8 percentage points; 95% CI, 15.6 to 28.0; P<0.001) with cabozantinib-atezolizumab.
Objective response was achieved in 36.5% vs 20.6% (difference, 15.9 percentage points; 95% CI, 8.2 to 23.6) with cemiplimab.
Objective response was achieved in 34% vs 32% (difference, 2 percentage points; 95% CI, -5 to 9) with epacadostat-pembrolizumab.
Objective response was achieved in 35.4% vs 7.2% (difference, 28.2 percentage points; 95% CI, 20.9 to 35.5; P<0.001) with sugemalimab.
Objective response was achieved in 73.0% vs 49.6% (difference, 23.4 percentage points; 95% CI, 13.5 to 33.3; P<0.001) with tislelizumab-chemotherapy.
Objective response was achieved in 51.9% vs 29.8% (difference, 22.1 percentage points; 95% CI, 11.1 to 33.1; P<0.001) with sintilimab-chemotherapy.
Objective response was achieved in 60.5% vs 39.0% (difference, 21.5 percentage points; 95% CI, 8.9 to 34.1; P=0.0008) with camrelizumab-chemotherapy.
Objective response was achieved in 44.7% vs 35.4% (difference, 9.3 percentage points; 95% CI, 0.8 to 17.8; P=0.03) with sintilimab.
